query: is BTK involved in any known autoimmune diseases

retrieved_docs.title: Bruton Agammaglobulinemia (Archived) -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: XLA is caused by mutations in the BTK gene which is present on the long arm of the X-chromosome. BTK is a member of the Tec family of nonreceptor protein-tyrosine kinases which are signal transduction molecules. Nearly 544 mutations have been associated with the disease. These include nonsense, splice site mutations as well as deletions and insertions. [4] [5] However, the single most common genetic event is a missense mutation. Different studies have shown that specific mutations may or may not correlate with the severity of the disease.

retrieved_docs.title: Bruton Agammaglobulinemia (Archived) -- Pearls and Other Issues
retrieved_docs.content: Even with lab testing a few hints supporting a diagnosis of XLA include CD19+ B cell numbers which are usually normal in most other causes of hypogammaglobulinemia/ agammaglobulinemia except ARA and some types of SCID. The former can be ruled out using genetic testing for mutations in the BTK gene while the latter also has abnormalities in the T-cell number.

retrieved_docs.title: X-Linked Agammaglobulinemia -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: Common aliases for X-linked agammaglobulinemia include Bruton agammaglobulinemia, Btk agammaglobulinemia, Bruton tyrosine kinase agammaglobulinemia, agammaglobulinemia of Bruton, and congenital agammaglobulinemia.

retrieved_docs.title: Agammaglobulinemia -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: X- linked agammaglobulinemia is caused by a mutation in the Bruton tyrosine kinase (BTK) gene, located on the long arm of the X-chromosome. BTK is a member of the Tec family and encodes for cytoplasmic non-receptor tyrosine kinases, which are signal transduction molecules. BTK is critical in the maturation of pre-B cells to mature B cells, a process that occurs in the bone marrow. [3] The disease has been associated with 544 mutations that include mainly missense mutations, insertions, deletions, and splice-site mutations. [4]

retrieved_docs.title: X-Linked Immunodeficiency -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: XLA disease caused by a mutation in the BTK gene has been named the Bruton gene to honor the clinician who diagnosed the first case. Approximately half of the patients are born in families previously diagnosed with XLA and this condition refers to the fact that the other half of the patients are contracted with the disease as a result of spontaneous mutations. B-cell development defect occurring in the patients as a result of BTK mutation results in decreased B-cell count in tissue and blood and reduced differentiation in plasma cell and ultimately in decreased levels of immunoglobulin along with the decreased specific antibody responses. [4] The absence of immunoglobulin involved in opsonization and neutralization of the encapsulated bacterias leads to susceptibility for infections caused by the encapsulated bacterias in particular. [14]

retrieved_docs.title: Agammaglobulinemia -- Pearls and Other Issues
retrieved_docs.content: Agammaglobulinemia is a rare disorder, and its diagnosis is often overlooked. Regardless of age, all males with hypogammaglobulinemia and low circulating B cells must be assessed for XLA by investigating the BTK gene. Certain varieties of XLA mimic other disorders, like CVID. The disease, which is characterized by a low count of B cells and reduced antibody production, is less severe in childhood and increases in intensity during adulthood.

retrieved_docs.title: X-Linked Agammaglobulinemia -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: Researchers have isolated several different mutations in the Btk gene. The finding of Btk gene mutations alone does not constitute a diagnosis. [10] [11] Findings which suggest a diagnosis of XLA in males whose B cell levels are below the 1 to 2% threshold include all or most of the following: A history of recurrent bacterial infections requiring one or more hospitalizations before the age of 5 years Poor humoral responses to vaccines No palpable tonsils or cervical lymph nodes Tests with abnormal results should be repeated by independent testing for confirmation before beginning treatment or seeking referrals.

retrieved_docs.title: X-Linked Agammaglobulinemia -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: The gene for Btk codes for a cytoplasmic tyrosine kinase protein, BTK, which acts as a signal transducer driving the final stages of B cell maturation. The inheritance of disease-causing mutation of the Btk gene interferes with BTK protein expression, resulting in the arrest of differentiation at the pre-B-cell stage in the bone marrow, causing a profound lack of mature B lymphocytes in the peripheral circulation and a corresponding absence or severe reduction in all immunoglobulin isotypes from the serum. [12]

retrieved_docs.title: Bruton Agammaglobulinemia (Archived) -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: These results point towards a probable diagnosis of XLA; however, to confirm the diagnosis, genetic testing to look for a mutation in BTK gene can be performed. A confirmed family history of XLA can serve as a surrogate for genetic testing.

retrieved_docs.title: X-Linked Agammaglobulinemia -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: B cell development is a process that occurs in the bone marrow, where pro-B cells develop into pre-B cells before fully mature B cells enter the peripheral circulation. Normally, pre-B cells express the pre-BCR complex, which undergoes activation by BTK to initiate downstream signaling events involved in the maturation process. This process becomes blocked at the pre-B cell stage in individuals with inherited mutations that prevent BTK expression (figure 1). Western blotting or flow cytometry analysis of BTK protein expression in the monocytes or platelets of individuals with XLA demonstrates that it cannot be detected. [8] [6]

retrieved_docs.title: X-Linked Agammaglobulinemia -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The molecular basis for XLA is a disruption in B cell development due to mutation in Bruton's tyrosine kinase (Btk). Affected individuals inherit a defect that prevents precursor B cells in the bone marrow from forming mature, circulating B-lymphocytes that would otherwise be capable of proliferating and differentiating into antibody-producing plasma cells in secondary lymphoid organs like the tonsils and lymph nodes. This dysfunction results in dangerously low, clinically undetectable levels of all immunoglobulin isotypes in the serum. [2] [3] [4]

retrieved_docs.title: X-Linked Agammaglobulinemia -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: A typical diagnostic test sequence would evaluate serum levels of IgG, IgM, and IgA, the number of CD19-positive or CD20-positive B cells in circulation, humoral vaccine responses, BTK protein expression in peripheral monocytes, and Btk gene sequencing. Test results consistent with a diagnosis of XLA in a male patient with a history of recurrent bacterial infections would include finding: Serum levels of IgG, IgM, and IgA that are more than two standard deviations below age-matched controls Absence of mature B lymphocytes in the peripheral circulation (i.e., fewer than 1-2%) Little or no increase in antibody titers 3-4 weeks after protein- or polysaccharide antigen vaccines (e.g., immunizing against pneumococcal pneumonia or diphtheria-tetanus) Low or absent BTK protein or mRNA expression levels Detection of disease-causing mutations in the Btk gene

retrieved_docs.title: Hypogammaglobulinemia -- Etiology -- X-linked Agammaglobulinemia (XLA) or Bruton Agammaglobulinemia
retrieved_docs.content: It is the first primary immune deficiency disease with a genetic cause identified. Mutation noted in the Bruton tyrosine kinase gene (Btk) is an important component for the maturation of pre-B cells. This leads to a lack of plasma cells with low levels of Ig and therefor no humoral response. [7] [8] Inheritance of primary agammaglobulinemia is X-linked, with most mutations being familial with few autosomal recessive forms. [9] Common Variable Immunodeficiency (CVID, V ariable Pan-hypogammaglobulinemia)

retrieved_docs.content: Relapsed or refractory chronic lymphocytic leukemia Small lymphocytic lymphoma Relapsed or refractory Mantle cell lymphoma Newly diagnosed non-germinal center B-cell subtype of diffuse large B-cell lymphoma

retrieved_docs.title: Hypogammaglobulinemia -- Pathophysiology -- X-linked Agammaglobulinemia (XLA) or Bruton Agammaglobulinemia
retrieved_docs.content: Bruton tyrosine kinase gene (Btk) is essential for the development of mature B-cells. Mutations in this gene will cause low levels of plasma cells and immunoglobulins resulting in little to no humoral response. [26] [8] This may cause immunoglobulin deficiency (hypogammaglobulinemia) or a complete absence of immunoglobulins (agammaglobulinemia). [18]

retrieved_docs.title: Immunoglobulin -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: X-linked agammaglobulinemia is also called Bruton agammaglobulinemia. It occurs due to a defect in Bruton Tyrosine Kinase (BTK) gene that prevents B-cell maturation. This condition is X-linked recessive and seen mostly in males. They present with recurrent bacterial and enteroviral infections after six months, once the maternal IgG is low. No B cells are seen in peripheral blood, and immunoglobulins of all classes are absent. Patients also have absent or scanty lymph nodes and tonsils. Live vaccines are contraindicated. [19]

retrieved_docs.content: Systemic lupus erythematosus (الذئبة الحمراء المجموعية) is clinically associated with other circulatory disease, and this category of circulatory disease is classified as a subtype of connective tissue disorder (اضطراب الأنسجة الضامة). This relationship underscores that cardiovascular manifestations observed in lupus are part of the broader spectrum of connective tissue diseases, highlighting the importance of integrated cardiovascular evaluation and management in patients with systemic lupus erythematosus.

The nonspecific symptom cluster “signs and symptoms of ill‑defined conditions” encompasses diseases and injuries, malaise and fatigue, and the Arabic term غشي, which also describes the clinical course of an asymptomatic basilar‑artery occlusion and is characterized as Transitory. Transient cerebral ischemia, also called نوبة إقفارية عابرة, is defined as a brief, transitory episode of neurological dysfunction. Risk factors (rizikové faktory) are noted for اضطراب الدم, emphasizing the need for their identification. Contraindicated agents for SLE and connective‑tissue disorders include trioxsalen and zlato. retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 3 -- Community 2276
retrieved_docs.content: The category of autoimmune disorders (اضطرابات مناعية/المناعة الذاتية) encompasses several distinct conditions, including التهاب المفاصل (arthritis), فقر الدم الانحلالي المكتسب (acquired hemolytic anemia), فقر الدم الانحلالي من النوع البارد (cold‑agglutinin hemolytic anemia), and التهاب الشريان التاجي (coronary artery inflammation). Each of these diseases is characterized by an inappropriate immune response that targets the body’s own tissues, leading to joint inflammation, destruction of red blood cells, or inflammation of the coronary arteries. Recognizing that these diverse clinical entities share a common autoimmune basis aids clinicians in diagnosing, monitoring, and applying immunomodulatory therapies across this spectrum of immune‑mediated illnesses.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 3 -- Community 2041
retrieved_docs.content: Aplastic anemia, non‑regenerative anemia, arthritis, hemolytic anemia that is not caused by autoimmunity, and coronary artery inflammation are all classified within the broader category of immune disorders/autoimmune conditions. This grouping reflects the shared underlying involvement of the immune system in the pathogenesis of these diverse diseases, whether through direct autoimmune attack on blood‑forming tissues, inflammatory processes affecting joints, or vascular inflammation of the coronary arteries. Recognizing these entities as members of the immune/autoimmune disorder spectrum helps clinicians consider common immunologic mechanisms, guide diagnostic evaluation, and select therapeutic strategies that target immune dysregulation across these seemingly disparate clinical presentations.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 3 -- Community 2420
retrieved_docs.content: Other circulatory disease is clinically associated with systemic lupus erythematosus (الذئبة الحمراء المجموعية), indicating that patients with systemic lupus erythematosus may experience or be predisposed to a range of circulatory system disorders. This relationship underscores the importance of cardiovascular assessment and monitoring in the management of systemic lupus erythematosus to identify and address potential circulatory complications promptly.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1795
retrieved_docs.content: The skin disorder حُمامَى is linked to other inflammatory conditions of the skin, indicating that it shares pathogenic mechanisms and clinical associations with additional inflammatory dermatoses. This “associated_with” relationship highlights the relevance of recognizing حُمامَى within the broader spectrum of skin inflammation for accurate diagnosis and comprehensive patient management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 3 -- Community 2241
retrieved_docs.content: Autoimmune disorders classified under اضطرابات مناعية/المناعة الذاتية (SMQ) encompass a diverse range of conditions that share an underlying immune‑mediated pathophysiology. فقر الدم الوبيل (warm autoimmune hemolytic anemia) is included in this group, reflecting the immune system’s attack on red blood cells leading to hemolysis. فقر الدم الانحلالي من النوع البارد (cold agglutinin disease) is also a member, representing a similar hemolytic process that is triggered by cold temperatures. التهاب المفاصل (arthritis) falls within the same SMQ category, illustrating how autoimmune mechanisms can target synovial joints and cause chronic inflammation and pain. Finally, التهاب الشريان التاجي (coronary artery inflammation) is part of اضطرابات مناعية/المناعة الذاتية (SMQ), highlighting that autoimmune activity can affect vascular structures and contribute to coronary artery disease. Recognizing these entities—warm and cold autoimmune hemolytic anemias, arthritis, and coronary artery inflammation—as members of a common autoimmune disorder group underscores their shared immunological basis and supports integrated diagnostic and therapeutic approaches in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 29 -- Level 3 -- Community 1539
retrieved_docs.content: Wiskott‑Aldrich anemia, aplastic anemia, arthritis, and cold‑type hemolytic anemia are all classified as members of the immune disorders/autoimmune (SMQ) category. By grouping these diverse conditions together, the classification highlights that each involves dysregulation of the immune system, whether through auto‑reactive mechanisms, immune‑mediated destruction of blood cells, or inflammatory joint disease. Recognizing these diseases as part of the broader autoimmune spectrum aids clinicians in understanding their shared pathophysiology, guiding diagnostic evaluation and informing therapeutic strategies that target immune pathways.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 28 -- Level 3 -- Community 2554
retrieved_docs.content: Other diseases of veins and lymphatics is a broad diagnostic category that encompasses both الاعتلال الوعائي (vascular disease) and اضطراب في جهاز الدورة الدموية غير محدد (unspecified circulatory system disorder). Within الاعتلال الوعائي, خثار الشريان القاعدي (basilar artery thrombosis) represents a specific form of vascular pathology. Within اضطراب في جهاز الدورة الدموية غير محدد, مرض قلبي روماتزمي مزمن (chronic rheumatic heart disease) is a distinct subtype. Recognizing these hierarchical relationships aids clinicians in classifying and managing venous, lymphatic, and circulatory disorders more effectively.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 2 -- Community 713
retrieved_docs.content: The category “اضطرابات مناعية/المناعة الذاتية (SMQ)” encompasses a range of hematologic and inflammatory conditions, including فقر الدم اللا تنسجي (aplastic anemia), فقر الدم الانحلالي المكتسب (acquired hemolytic anemia), فقر دم انحلالي ليس ناجمًا عن المناعة الذاتية (hemolytic anemia not caused by autoimmunity), التهاب المفاصل (arthritis), and التهاب الشريان التاجي (coronary artery inflammation). These entities are grouped together because they involve immune system dysregulation, whether through autoantibody‑mediated attacks, cell‑mediated immune injury, or broader immune‑related inflammatory processes. While most of the listed disorders are driven by autoimmune mechanisms, the non‑autoimmune hemolytic anemia is included in the classification for organizational purposes despite its different pathogenesis. Recognizing that these diverse diseases share an underlying immune component helps clinicians identify common diagnostic pathways and consider immunomodulatory therapies across different organ systems, thereby improving patient assessment and management.
